title: thismia sahyadrica
intro: Thismia sahyadrica, also known as the Indian pipewort, is a rare and endemic species of flowering plant found in the Western Ghats of India. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Thismia sahyadrica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and poultices.
